title: Canker sore
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Canker sores are painful mouth ulcers that usually heal on their own.
Home care may include gentle oral hygiene, avoiding irritating foods, and short-term over-the-counter pain relief when safe.
Most pain improves in a few days and the sore usually resolves within about 10 to 14 days.
Patients should contact a clinician if an ulcer persists beyond two weeks, worsens, recurs often, or occurs with fever, diarrhea, headache, or rash.
